preview_text:
The model favors Netherlands at 70% to win, with Tunisia given an 8.5% chance of victory. The underdog faces a substantial gap: a 61-percentage-point differential separates the two teams' win probabilities.

The draw carries meaningful weight at 22%, suggesting the match is unlikely to be a rout. The asymmetry in the W/D/L spread—with Netherlands heavily favored but a draw plausible and Tunisia not eliminated—indicates a competitive fixture rather than a mismatch, though the model marks clear separation in underlying quality.

Model and market are closely aligned across all three outcomes. The sportsbook consensus (72% Netherlands, 18% draw, 10% Tunisia) sits within 2–3 percentage points of the model on each line. Polymarket prices show similar agreement. The broad consensus indicates confidence in the directional forecast without sharp disagreement among sources.
